title: Logs may generate change proposals but never automatic patches
summary: HermesOps may analyze sanitized runtime and audit logs to identify findings and propose changes, but logs must never trigger automatic code or configuration changes without human review.
facts: Logs can contain sensitive operational details.
Only sanitized logs may be analyzed for MemoryOS candidates.
Automatic changes from logs can introduce unsafe behavior.
inferences: A governed change proposal workflow is required before applying any log-derived change.
decisions: No log-derived change may be applied automatically.
rollback: Revert plugin log policy if corporate policy changes.
